description: Fibonacci numbers F(0)..F(100) with one short fact per index, drawn from history, science, computer science, science fiction, mathematics, and culture.

entries.fact: Brahmagupta defined arithmetic with zero in his Brahmasphutasiddhanta (628 CE) — the earliest text to treat zero as a true number.
In binary, 1 is the only nonzero digit. Every byte you've ever sent is a pattern of 1s and 0s.
The repeated 1 is what makes Fibonacci self-starting: F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2) needs two seeds, and both happen to be 1.
Two is the only even prime — every other even number is divisible by 2, so it can't be prime.
Three primary colors (RGB) generate every color on your screen by additive light mixing.
There are exactly five Platonic solids — proven complete by Euclid in Elements, Book XIII (~300 BCE).
A byte equals 8 bits, standardized by IBM's System/360 in 1964. Before that, 'byte' could mean 6, 7, or 9 bits.
ISBN-13 replaced ISBN-10 worldwide on January 1, 2007, expanding the global book-identifier address space.
Blackjack ('21') is the world's most-played banking card game. The house edge comes from players acting before the dealer.
Wolfram's elementary cellular automaton Rule 34 evolves to a stable striped pattern from any starting state — a 'class 2' rule.
The U.S. national speed limit was set to 55 mph in 1974 during the OPEC oil crisis, via the Emergency Highway Energy Conservation Act.
F(11)=89 is a Fibonacci prime — and the reciprocal 1/89 = 0.01123595... encodes the Fibonacci sequence in its decimal expansion.
A gross equals 144 — a dozen dozen — used in medieval and modern packaging because 12 has more divisors than 10.
F(13)=233 is a Fibonacci prime, and uranium-233 is a fissile isotope bred from thorium for advanced reactor fuel cycles.
The Boeing 377 Stratocruiser (1947) was a luxury double-decker airliner with a cocktail lounge on the lower deck.
In 610 CE, Muhammad is said to have received his first revelation in the cave of Hira — the traditional start of Islam.
In 987 CE, Hugh Capet was crowned King of the Franks, founding the Capetian dynasty that ruled France for centuries.
F(17)=1597 is a Fibonacci prime — only a handful are known, and finding more is an open problem in number theory.
Eighteen is the global age of legal majority in most countries — the moment civic identity formally begins.
F(19)=4181 looks like it should be prime (its index is) but it isn't — it factors as 37 × 113, a famous Fibonacci counterexample.
F(20)=6765. The next time a Fibonacci number gains a whole new digit-decade won't be for several more steps — exponential growth becomes visible here.
21 is the U.S. legal drinking age and the magic number in blackjack. F(21)=10,946 — the smallest Fibonacci above ten thousand.
Joseph Heller's Catch-22 (1961) gave the world a name for paradoxical bureaucratic logic: rules that defeat themselves.
F(23)=28,657 is a Fibonacci prime. Michael Jordan wore #23 because his older brother Larry wore #45 — half plus one, rounded up.
There are 24 hours in a day — a convention from ancient Egypt, who used 12 daytime + 12 nighttime hours that varied with the seasons.
A silver jubilee marks 25 years. Queen Elizabeth II's silver jubilee in 1977 drew street parties across the Commonwealth.
The English alphabet has 26 letters; Hawaiian uses just 13 — exactly half.
The '27 Club' is the morbid pop-culture grouping of musicians who died at 27 — Hendrix, Joplin, Morrison, Cobain, Winehouse.
The lunar cycle is ~29.5 days, but folklore and pre-modern calendars often round it to 28 — close enough to a menstrual cycle to fuel ancient symbolism.
F(29)=514,229 is a Fibonacci prime. Saturn takes ~29.5 Earth years to orbit the Sun — about one Saturnian year per human generation.
The Thirty Years' War (1618–1648) reshaped Europe and ended with the Peace of Westphalia, the basis of modern state sovereignty.
31 = 2⁵ − 1 is a Mersenne prime; Mersenne primes are the source of every known even perfect number.
32-bit computing dominated personal computers from the mid-1980s until 64-bit went mainstream around 2008.
At 33, Alexander the Great died in Babylon (323 BCE) having conquered most of the known world from Greece to the Indus.
Dwight D. Eisenhower was the 34th U.S. President and signed the Federal-Aid Highway Act of 1956, creating the Interstate System.
The U.S. Constitution sets 35 as the minimum age to be President — a deliberate floor for maturity and experience.
The film The 36th Chamber of Shaolin (1978) inspired Wu-Tang Clan's 1993 debut album Enter the Wu-Tang (36 Chambers).
37signals (now Basecamp) popularized the 'less software' philosophy and gave the world Ruby on Rails in 2004.
The 38th parallel divided Korea after WWII and remains the boundary between North and South to this day.
Hitchcock's The 39 Steps (1935) pioneered the 'wrong man on the run' thriller template still copied today.
The biblical '40 days and 40 nights' appears in the stories of Noah, Moses, and Jesus — symbolizing trial and transformation.
George H. W. Bush, the 41st U.S. President, was the last WWII veteran to hold the office.
In Douglas Adams' Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, 42 is the answer to 'the Ultimate Question of Life, the Universe, and Everything.'
F(43)=433,494,437 is a Fibonacci prime — the 9th known.
Barack Obama, the 44th U.S. President, was the first born in Hawaii — and the first Black president of the United States.
The 45 RPM vinyl single, introduced by RCA in 1949, became the format of pop music for the next two decades.
Joe Biden, the 46th U.S. President, was the oldest person to assume the office, at age 78.
F(47)=2,971,215,073 is a Fibonacci prime — the 10th known. Fibonacci primes get rarer as the index grows.
The 'Lower 48' — the contiguous U.S. states excluding Alaska and Hawaii — is American shorthand for the mainland.
The '49ers' rushed to California in 1849, swelling its non-Native population from ~14,000 to ~250,000 in just four years.
The U.S. has 50 states. A golden jubilee marks 50 years. F(50)=12,586,269,025 — Fibonacci has now passed 10 billion.
Area 51, a U.S. Air Force facility in Nevada, was officially acknowledged by the CIA only in 2013, ending decades of denial.
A standard playing-card deck has 52 cards — one for each week of the year, possibly by design.
Asteroid 53 Kalypso was discovered in 1858, named for the Greek nymph who held Odysseus on her island for seven years.
Studio 54 (1977–1980) defined the disco era of New York nightlife before its tax-evasion-driven collapse.
F(55)=139,583,862,445. And 55 is itself a Fibonacci number — F(10) — making this F(F(10)), a doubly-Fibonacci position.
The 56k modem (V.90 standard, 1998) was the last hurrah of dial-up before broadband took over the home internet.
'Heinz 57' was a marketing slogan from 1896. Heinz actually made far more than 57 products — the founder just liked the number.
The Voyager Golden Record (1977) carries 55 different greetings to potential extraterrestrial listeners — close to 58, the index here.
The 1959 Antarctic Treaty designated Antarctica as a scientific preserve and banned military activity — the Cold War's first arms-control treaty.
The Babylonians used base-60, which is why we have 60 seconds, 60 minutes, and 360 degrees in a circle.
Bob Dylan's Highway 61 Revisited (1965) — referencing the road from his Minnesota hometown — went electric and split the folk world.
Average global ocean surface temperature is around 62°F (17°C). Small changes here drive massive shifts in climate.
63 = 2⁶ − 1 is a Mersenne number, but NOT a Mersenne prime — it factors as 7 × 9.
A chessboard has 64 squares — and the wheat-and-chessboard legend asks for 2⁶⁴−1 grains, more than humanity has ever harvested.
65 mph is the most common U.S. interstate speed limit, and 65 is the typical retirement age across much of the developed world.
Route 66 — the 'Mother Road' — ran 2,448 miles from Chicago to Santa Monica before being decommissioned in 1985.
Comet 67P/Churyumov–Gerasimenko is where ESA's Rosetta mission landed the Philae probe in 2014 — humanity's first comet landing.
The 1968 Apollo 8 mission was the first time humans orbited the Moon and saw Earth rise above another world.
The Apollo 11 Moon landing happened in 1969 — Neil Armstrong's 'small step' was watched live by ~650 million people.
70mm film, used for IMAX and epics like Lawrence of Arabia, captures roughly 4× the resolution of standard 35mm.
Lutetium (atomic number 71) is the densest of the lanthanides and is named after Lutetia, the Roman name for Paris.
In Kabbalah, the Shem HaMephorash — the 72-fold name of God — is derived from three verses of Exodus 14.
On The Big Bang Theory, Sheldon calls 73 'the best number': it's prime, its mirror 37 is prime, and 73 in binary (1001001) is a palindrome.
Asteroid 74 Galatea was discovered in 1862. In Greek myth, Galatea is the statue Pygmalion sculpted and fell in love with.
A diamond jubilee marks 75 (or sometimes 60) years — among the rarest of anniversaries to celebrate.
The 'Spirit of '76' refers to 1776 — the year of the U.S. Declaration of Independence; the bicentennial in 1976 sparked a wave of patriotic art.
77 Sunset Strip (1958–1964) was the first hour-long detective show on American TV and made Edd 'Kookie' Byrnes a teen idol.
The 78 RPM record dominated music from 1898 to the late 1950s before being replaced by the 33⅓ LP and the 45 single.
Gold has atomic number 79; its symbol Au comes from Latin aurum, meaning 'shining dawn.'
Jules Verne's Around the World in Eighty Days (1872) anticipated the era of global travel made possible by steamships and the Suez Canal.
81 = 3⁴, the smallest fourth power above 1; it appears in classical Chinese thought as the number of chapters in the Tao Te Ching.
The 82nd Airborne Division ('All American') jumped into Normandy on D-Day, June 6, 1944.
Bismuth (atomic number 83) was long thought to be the heaviest stable element — until 2003, when its alpha-decay half-life was measured at ~10¹⁹ years.
George Orwell's Nineteen Eighty-Four (1949) gave us 'Big Brother,' 'doublethink,' 'thoughtcrime,' and 'Newspeak.'
At index 85, Fibonacci numbers cross 18 digits — they now exceed the global count of every grain of sand on Earth (~7.5 × 10¹⁸).
'86' as a verb — meaning to refuse service or eject — is American diner slang from the 1920s–30s, possibly rhyming with 'nix.'
Lincoln's Gettysburg Address opens 'Four score and seven years ago' — 87 years before 1863 was 1776, the Declaration of Independence.
A standard piano has 88 keys — 52 white and 36 black — covering 7¼ octaves from A0 to C8.
In 1989, the Berlin Wall fell on November 9, ending 28 years of dividing the city — and effectively the Cold War.
A right angle is 90°. The right triangle and its 3-4-5 ratio is the foundation of all surveying and navigation.
91 = 7 × 13. It looks prime but isn't — a famous 'almost prime' that fools people in mental-math tests.
Uranium has atomic number 92, the heaviest element found in significant quantities in nature.
The 1993 release of Mosaic, the first popular graphical web browser, is when 'the Internet' became visible to non-academics.
In 1994, Linux 1.0 was released — Linus Torvalds' kernel that now runs most servers, every Android phone, and every Top500 supercomputer.
Martin Luther's 95 Theses, posted at the Wittenberg church in 1517, lit the fuse of the Protestant Reformation.
Curium (atomic number 96) was first synthesized in 1944 at the Berkeley Radiation Laboratory and named for Marie and Pierre Curie.
Apple's 1997 'Think Different' campaign launched Steve Jobs' return-era branding and saved the company from near-bankruptcy.
Normal human body temperature is 98.6°F (37°C) — a 19th-century average that varies by ~1°F across individuals and time of day.
Nena's '99 Luftballons' (1983) tells of party balloons mistaken for a missile attack — a Cold War parable in pop form.
F(100) = 354,224,848,179,261,915,075 — a 21-digit number. It was first computed by hand long before computers existed.
